issues.description: J-type immediate sign-extension width mismatch: J-type immediate has 21 bits of actual data (instr[31]+instr[19:12]+instr[20]+instr[30:21]+1'b0 = 1+8+1+10+1 = 21 bits), requiring 11 bits of sign extension for 32-bit result. Current code uses 20 bits of sign extension, producing a 33-bit concatenation that gets truncated, causing incorrect immediate values for JAL instructions with negative offsets.
issues.fix_suggestion: Change line 29 from `assign j_imm = {{20{instr[31]}}, instr[31], instr[19:12],` to `assign j_imm = {{11{instr[31]}}, instr[31], instr[19:12],`

issues.description: B-type immediate sign-extension width mismatch: B-type immediate has 13 bits of actual data (instr[31]+instr[7]+instr[30:25]+instr[11:8]+1'b0 = 1+1+6+4+1 = 13 bits), requiring 19 bits of sign extension for 32-bit result. Current code uses 20 bits of sign extension, producing a 33-bit concatenation that gets truncated, causing incorrect branch offsets for negative branches.
issues.fix_suggestion: Change line 22 from `assign b_imm = {{20{instr[31]}}, instr[31], instr[7],` to `assign b_imm = {{19{instr[31]}}, instr[31], instr[7],`

summary: The imm_gen module has two critical bugs in sign-extension width calculations. The J-type and B-type immediates use 20 bits of sign extension when they should use 11 and 19 bits respectively, due to incorrect accounting of the actual immediate field widths (21 bits for J-type, 13 bits for B-type). This results in 33-bit concatenations that get truncated to 32 bits, causing incorrect immediate values for JAL instructions and branch instructions with negative offsets. I-type (12 bits), S-type (12 bits), and U-type (20 bits) immediate extractions are correct. The opcode-based case statement coverage and default case handling are proper.
